title: Specify adapter manifest
description: Shared shape for source and target adapter manifests (`adapter.yaml`) per RFC-25 §Adapter implementation shape. Carries the kebab-case `name`, integer `version`, the `axis` discriminator (`source` or `target`), the closed list of `operations`, the `briefs.<operation>` map of relative brief paths, and an optional `tools[]` declaration. Source and target schemas refine this shape with axis-specific `operations` and `briefs` constraints.

name.description: Adapter identifier in kebab-case. Must match the directory name under sources/ or targets/.

version.description: Major adapter version. Increments when the adapter ships breaking operation or contract changes.

axis.description: Adapter role. `source` adapters enumerate candidates and extract evidence; `target` adapters supply `shape`, `build`, and `merge` briefs.

operations.description: Closed list of operations this adapter declares. Source adapters: `enumerate`, `extract`. Target adapters: `shape`, `build`, `merge`.

briefs.description: Map from operation name (matching an `operations[]` entry) to a relative path to the brief markdown file. Absolute paths and URIs are rejected.

tools.description: Optional WASI tool declarations per RFC-15. Tools resolve into `.specify/.cache/{sources,targets}/<name>/`.

description.description: Optional human-readable, single-sentence summary of the adapter's role.

cache.description: RFC-27 §D8 — optional cache opt-out switch. When set to `opt-out`, the CLI treats every `extract` (and `enumerate`) run for this adapter as a cache miss; the `slice.extract.cache-miss` journal event carries `reason: adapter-opt-out`. Absence (the default) means cache fingerprinting is on. Intended for adapters whose intermediate results are non-deterministic; new values require an RFC update.

kebabName.description: Kebab-case identifier: lowercase letters and digits grouped by single internal hyphens; no leading, trailing, or consecutive hyphens.
